title: Functions, Multiple Results, Closures, Recursion, and Contracts · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A rule engine needs small composable functions, explicit failure results, isolated stateful closures, and bounded recursive traversal. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a composable rule and transformation library after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design function signatures, named types, parameters, results, and first-class function values around one responsibility.
Use multiple results and the blank identifier without discarding failure or evidence that the caller needs.
Define and call variadic functions, distinguish the slice parameter, and forward existing slices with explicit ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Recursion Bounds
steps.instruction:
review case — Build 3 independent rule closures with thresholds 15 and 21; preserve multiple-result failure evidence, forward one variadic slice deliberately, and prove recursion reaches a bounded base case. Competency probe: Use recursion only with a demonstrable base case, progress measure, depth bound, and iterative comparison.

Inspect the Recursion Bounds trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Recursion Bounds.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use recursion only with a demonstrable base case, progress measure, depth bound, and iterative comparison. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Recursion Bounds, keep Recursion Bounds connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
